evidence_quotes.quote: you buy a product but we tell you it is only a license which we can revoke at any time

paragraphs.text: Good day. Besides the comments comming in at the moment about video games, I want to stress, that the whole 'Products sold as Licenses' situation is in strong disadvantage to the commen consumer. What used to be purchases that could be used whenever the consumer wanted (like good old CDs), nowadays this is often to the mercy of the producer. Just look at the early Photoshop versions that moved to the cloud: Once Adobe no longer wished to serve the program files, they discontinued the servers, essentially disowning anyone that purchased a license (without expiration date!) of that specific versions (because the program was no longer accessible). So, please, regulate the whole 'you buy a product but we tell you it is only a license which we can revoke at any time' mess. Thank you.
